audition_requirements.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: One movement of a standard concerto; Two movements of a Bach suite | Audition: One movement of a standard concerto - if you plan to play the first movement of either the Elgar: Concerto in E minor or Shostakovich: Cello Concerto No. 1, please also prepare the second movement; One complete étude by a composer such as Duport, Popper, etc.; Two movements of a Bach suite; One piece of the applicant's choice; The faculty encourages the applicant to choose a work by composers from historically underrepresented groups or a work of their own composition. Compositions chosen should demonstrate the applicant's ability in phrasing, sustained playing, and technical facility, including legato and staccato at varied tempi. In recorded audition materials, piano accompaniment for the concerto is preferred but not required.

prescreen: One movement of a standard concerto
Two movements of a Bach suite
audition: One movement of a standard concerto - if you plan to play the first movement of either the Elgar: Concerto in E minor or Shostakovich: Cello Concerto No. 1, please also prepare the second movement
One complete étude by a composer such as Duport, Popper, etc.
Two movements of a Bach suite
One piece of the applicant's choice

The faculty encourages the applicant to choose a work by composers from historically underrepresented groups or a work of their own composition. Compositions chosen should demonstrate the applicant's ability in phrasing, sustained playing, and technical facility, including legato and staccato at varied tempi. In recorded audition materials, piano accompaniment for the concerto is preferred but not required.

audition_requirements.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: Not to exceed 10 minutes of music.; Applicant can choose one or multiple works.; Bb or A clarinet is acceptable for any work. If you would like to perform a solo or excerpts on the bass clarinet or E-flat clarinet, it would be in addition to the requirements above. Piano accompaniment not required but appreciated.; The selected work(s) should demonstrate the following criteria to the best of your ability: creativity, lyrical playing, technical facility and the passion for the instrument and repertoire you have chosen to perform. Please announce your name at the beginning of the video. | Audition: Two required works:; Mozart’s Clarinet Concerto , first movement, exposition only: m. 57-m. 155; Samuel Coleridge-Taylor's Clarinet Quintet Op.10 , second movement, begin at 5th bar of letter O and end at 3rd bar of Letter P; One of the following works:; Antonio Lauro's Lauro for Clarinet , arranged by Paquito D'Rivera, choose one movement; Stravinsky's Three Pieces for Solo Clarinet , Mvt. II only; Zach Good '13, Madder and woad (2020), a concert étude for solo Bb clarinet dedicated to the memory of Oberlin student Jung Choi (2012); One orchestral excerpt:; Mendelssohn's A Midsummer Night’s Dream , Scherzo, from beginning to bar 48; Bb or A clarinet is acceptable for any work. If you would like to perform a solo or excerpts on the bass clarinet or E-flat clarinet, it would be in addition to the requirements above. Piano accompaniment is optional.

prescreen: Not to exceed 10 minutes of music.
Applicant can choose one or multiple works.

Bb or A clarinet is acceptable for any work. If you would like to perform a solo or excerpts on the bass clarinet or E-flat clarinet, it would be in addition to the requirements above. Piano accompaniment not required but appreciated.

The selected work(s) should demonstrate the following criteria to the best of your ability: creativity, lyrical playing, technical facility and the passion for the instrument and repertoire you have chosen to perform. Please announce your name at the beginning of the video.
audition: Two required works:
Mozart’s Clarinet Concerto , first movement, exposition only: m. 57-m. 155
Samuel Coleridge-Taylor's Clarinet Quintet Op.10 , second movement, begin at 5th bar of letter O and end at 3rd bar of Letter P
One of the following works:
Antonio Lauro's Lauro for Clarinet , arranged by Paquito D'Rivera, choose one movement
Stravinsky's Three Pieces for Solo Clarinet , Mvt. II only
Zach Good '13, Madder and woad (2020), a concert étude for solo Bb clarinet dedicated to the memory of Oberlin student Jung Choi (2012)
One orchestral excerpt:
Mendelssohn's A Midsummer Night’s Dream , Scherzo, from beginning to bar 48

Bb or A clarinet is acceptable for any work. If you would like to perform a solo or excerpts on the bass clarinet or E-flat clarinet, it would be in addition to the requirements above. Piano accompaniment is optional.

audition_requirements.repertoire_summary: Prescreen: One composition by Bach; A sonata-allegro movement from a classical sonata; One étude of virtuosity; One other piece of the applicant’s choice; Ten minutes of music of the applicant's choice | Audition: When choosing repertoire to satisfy requirements of your choice, we encourage exploring the works of composers from historically underrepresented groups, or other works that you find personally meaningful or diverse. Choosing repertoire is an opportunity to showcase works that best reflect your culture, gender or other forms of self-identity. Check the Oberlin Conservatory Library webpage Diversify Your Repertoire for ideas to get started.; Early Decision: Saturday, December 5, 2026*; Saturday, February 6, 2027; Sunday, February 7, 2027*; Saturday, February 13, 2027; Sunday, February 14, 2027; *Professor Dang Thai Son is only available for on-campus auditions for these dates.; One composition by Bach; A complete classical sonata (Mozart, Haydn, Beethoven); One étude of virtuosity; One composition from the romantic period; One composition by a 20th or 21st century composer; Early Decision: Saturday, December 5, 2026; Requests to waive the screening requirement may be submitted by applicants who have previously played for the organ faculty or attended the Oberlin Summer Organ Academy.; Three or four contrasting pieces of organ literature that demonstrate the applicant's technical and musical skills; Sight-reading of a four-part hymn harmonization; At least one piece should be chosen from the works of J.S. Bach. Works by women and other minority-represented composers are encouraged. Admission to the organ program for pianists with limited organ background will be considered on a case by case basis. These applicants may include a partial audition on the piano.; Three or four contrasting pieces of organ literature that demonstrate the applicant's technical and musical skills. One work must be a major prelude and fugue or trio sonata of Johann Sebastian Bach, and one work must be from the pre-Bach era. The remaining selections may be freely chosen.

prescreen: One composition by Bach
A sonata-allegro movement from a classical sonata
One étude of virtuosity
One other piece of the applicant’s choice
Ten minutes of music of the applicant's choice
audition: When choosing repertoire to satisfy requirements of your choice, we encourage exploring the works of composers from historically underrepresented groups, or other works that you find personally meaningful or diverse. Choosing repertoire is an opportunity to showcase works that best reflect your culture, gender or other forms of self-identity. Check the Oberlin Conservatory Library webpage Diversify Your Repertoire for ideas to get started.
Early Decision: Saturday, December 5, 2026*
Saturday, February 6, 2027
Sunday, February 7, 2027*
Saturday, February 13, 2027
Sunday, February 14, 2027
*Professor Dang Thai Son is only available for on-campus auditions for these dates.
One composition by Bach
A complete classical sonata (Mozart, Haydn, Beethoven)
One étude of virtuosity
One composition from the romantic period
One composition by a 20th or 21st century composer
Early Decision: Saturday, December 5, 2026
Requests to waive the screening requirement may be submitted by applicants who have previously played for the organ faculty or attended the Oberlin Summer Organ Academy.
Three or four contrasting pieces of organ literature that demonstrate the applicant's technical and musical skills
Sight-reading of a four-part hymn harmonization

At least one piece should be chosen from the works of J.S. Bach. Works by women and other minority-represented composers are encouraged. Admission to the organ program for pianists with limited organ background will be considered on a case by case basis. These applicants may include a partial audition on the piano.

Three or four contrasting pieces of organ literature that demonstrate the applicant's technical and musical skills. One work must be a major prelude and fugue or trio sonata of Johann Sebastian Bach, and one work must be from the pre-Bach era. The remaining selections may be freely chosen.

prescreen: Early Decision: December 5, 2026
Saturday, February 6, 2027
Saturday, February 13, 2027
Sunday, February 14*, 2027
*Please note Professor Derek Zadinsky will not be present on this date but will view a recording of your audition.
Two or more selections from final audition requirements listed below, totaling approximately ten minutes of playing
Choose three of the following bulleted requirements:
Two contrasting movements (or pieces) from the concerto, sonata, or similar repertoire
One or two movements from any of the Bach Cello Suites or any comparable piece or movement composed before 1750
Three contrasting excerpts from the standard orchestral repertoire
One piece composed after 1950, may be by a living composer
The faculty encourages the applicant to choose a work by composers from historically underrepresented groups or a work of their own composition.
audition: Choose three of the following bulleted requirements:
Two contrasting movements (or pieces) from the concerto, sonata, or similar repertoire
One or two movements from any of the Bach Cello Suites or any comparable piece or movement composed before 1750
Three contrasting excerpts from the standard orchestral repertoire
One piece composed after 1950, may be by a living composer
The faculty encourages the applicant to choose a work by composers from historically underrepresented groups or a work of their own composition.

Sunday, February 7, 2027
Saturday, February 13, 2027
Sunday, February 14, 2027
Below you will find the screening and final audition requirements for each of the three required areas, followed by examples.

Percussion education can take many paths. Professor Karre is committed to an open and inclusive environment, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender, or LGBTQ+ status, where all approaches are valid and young artists can build rewarding lives as creative musicians and thoughtful collaborators. If the examples below are unfamiliar to you, Professor Karre would still like to hear you play and understand your goals as a percussionist.
Recommendation: When choosing music for your audition select works that are slightly below your technical level, so that your interpretation is clear and expressive.
Screening Requirements:
One composition, or movement with four mallets
One composition, or movement with two mallets
Final Audition Requirements:
Example works:

2 Mallet J.S. Bach - one movement from Sonatas and Partitas for Solo Violin J.S. Bach - one movement from Six Suites for Solo Cello 4 Mallet Keiko Abe - Dream of the Cherry Blossoms Pius Cheung - Etude in E-minor Joanne Metcalf - Floating and Grooving Kaori Okatani - Three Colors Gordon Stout - Two Mexican Dances Toshimitsu Tanaka - Two Movements for Marimba Errolyn Wallen - Remember, Marimba Andrea Venet - ​Strobilanthes
You may perform passages from orchestra, wind ensemble, band, marching band (front ensemble) which include 2 and 4 mallet playing on xylophone, marimba, or vibraphone.
Two études of diverse styles demonstrating musicality as well as technical ability
Or
You may perform passages from orchestra, wind ensemble, band, or marching band that include drums performed with sticks.
Two études or compositions demonstrating musicality as well as technical ability
Example works: Anthony Cirone - Portraits in Rhythm Jacques Delécluse - Douze Études pour Caisse Claire John S. Pratt - 14 Modern Contest Solos Charles Wilcoxon - Modern Rudimental Swing Solos
There are no timpani screening requirements.
One composition or movement from a composition
Demonstration of tuning ability
You may perform passages from wind ensemble, band, or marching band (front ensemble) that include timpani.
